etymology.description: semantic 土(土) (tǔ, "earth") + phonetic 也(也) (yě) -> earth
etymology.interpretation: The character 地 (dì, "earth") combines the semantic element 土(土) (tǔ, "earth") suggesting the concept of ground or soil, with the phonetic element 也(也) (yě) providing the sound. This construction intuitively links the meaning of earth or ground with its pronunciation.

examples.chinese: 小狗在地上打滚(小狗在地上打滾)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo gǒu zài dì shàng dǎgǔn.
examples.english: The puppy is rolling on the ground.

examples.chinese: 地震后(地震後)，居民们迅速疏散(居民們迅速疏散)。
examples.pinyin: Dìzhèn hòu, jūmín men xùnsù shūsàn.
examples.english: After the earthquake, the residents quickly evacuated.

examples.chinese: 农民们正在田地里工作(農民們正在田地裡工作)。
examples.pinyin: Nóngmín men zhèngzài tiándì lǐ gōngzuò.
examples.english: The farmers are working in the fields.
